title: China Remains Uniquely Positioned to Carry Out Stimulus
section_path: China Remains Uniquely Positioned to Carry Out Stimulus
section_path_text: China Remains Uniquely Positioned to Carry Out Stimulus
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly China Report, inflation in China most recently came in at 0.5% in January. This is the highest inflation seen in China since January, but it is still relatively low compared to other major economies. Image reference: $1.jpg | assets/2025-02-28_china-remains-uniquely-positioned-to-carry-out-stimulus_img_241_51d63808a3af.jpg
Inflation in the United States, for example, came in at 3% in January.
